meta.title: Part 5 — extra practice set (30 items)
meta.notes: Extra bank for app (30 items, exam-style count); verify answers before study.

items.prompt: The vendor agreed to ------- the delivery schedule after the port reopened.
items.explanation: Agree to + bare infinitive revise; revision is noun; revises wrong after to.
analysisSteps: This Grammar - Word Form item tests the blank where wording around vendor and agreed plus delivery from the same sentence forces one licensed choice among the four listed options.
Anchor on "The vendor agreed to" near the gap because that visible chunk of the prompt signals what structural role the missing word must satisfy beside vendor in context.
A tempting trap is option C when "revising" sounds plausible near agreed, yet it does not lock onto the same clause pattern the sentence builds around delivery.
Option B with "revises" fails the slot test for independent reasons; option D using "revision" also misaligns with the matrix clause beside the blank, unlike A.
The correct string is "revise" for this gap; compare "revises" from B which cannot carry the same grammatical weight as the keyed answer tied to vendor here.

items.prompt: The committee will not ------- the merger until regulators publish their guidance.

Option B with "finalizing" fails the slot test for independent reasons; option D using "finalization" also misaligns with the matrix clause beside the blank, unlike A.
The correct string is "finalize" for this gap; compare "finalizing" from B which cannot carry the same grammatical weight as the keyed answer tied to committee here.

items.prompt: ------- the pilot exceeded expectations, the steering group approved a second funding round.

The correct string is "Although" for this gap; compare "Because" from A which cannot carry the same grammatical weight as the keyed answer tied to pilot here.
